rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes for port-level contracts, and in implementation annexes for subcomponent-level bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model to avoid introducing undefined references.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be scoped to valid AADL component types or implementations based on the nature of the assumptions or guarantees.
Ensure that all identifiers in AGREE annexes are declared or visible in the AADL model to prevent undefined reference errors.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` and should follow AADL scope rules for component types and implementations.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference component ports and data types that are explicitly defined in the AADL model.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-specific bindings in component type annexes unless they are represented through valid variables or outputs.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers or data types that are not present in the analyzed AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax and annex placement rules
